review_questions: Does CP00-144 cover exactly the 150 planned Risk C fixture and permission matrix reference units in order?
Can any CP00-144 fixture row load payloads, materialize manifests, persist stable IDs, emit receipts, run replay, execute AI retrieval, or analytics queries?

Can any CP00-144 permission matrix row evaluate view, search, mutation, export/download, share, AI retrieval, legal hold, ethical wall, object ACL, review, approval, trimming, or audit event behavior?
Are hidden source fields, unauthorized counts, object names, internal permission rules, ACL grants, and route reasons suppressed?
Does CP00-144 hand off cleanly from CP00-143 to CP00-145 without implementing LDIP or splitting HRX?

parsed_review.adjudication_notes:
CP00-144 is a Risk C no-write descriptor pack and passes all five review foci.

(1) Coverage and order: The nine micro-phase generators sum to exactly 150 units: P05.M06 (start=2, 19), P05.M07 (22), P05.M08 (20), P05.M09 (20), P05.M10 (8), P06.M00 (11), P06.M01 (11), P06.M02 (20), P06.M03 (19) = 150. Unit IDs are strictly ascending from RP03.P05.M06.S02 to RP03.P06.M03.S19 and unique (distinct micro_phase prefixes plus distinct S-indices). validateAuditComplianceCp144Coverage checks count, first/last, uniqueness, and per-index order against the plan's included_units; the supplied node --test (50/50 pass) and npm run rp03:audit:validate (covered_units 150, current_pack CP00-144) confirm catalog order equals plan order. Deliverable and evidence-mode distributions recompute exactly to the asserted totals (fixture 33, claude_review 7, implementation 59, security_audit 22, test 15, hermes_evidence 4, ui 10 = 150; nine evidence modes 19+22+20+20+8+11+11+20+19 = 150).

(2) Fixture rows cannot execute: every run-case result flows through freezeNoWriteResult, which forces loads_fixture_payload, materializes_fixture_manifest/golden/failure_payload, executes_replay_command, persists_stable_id, emits_real_receipt, executes_ai_retrieval, and executes_analytics_query to false AFTER spreading the input object, so caller input cannot override the attestation. All functions are pure (no fs/child_process/network/eval); the Hermes packet labels supplied command strings without running them.

(3) Permission rows cannot evaluate: evaluates_permission_matrix/view/search/mutation/export_download/share/ai_retrieval_decision, applies_legal_hold/ethical_wall, reads_object_acl, routes_review_required/approval_required, proves_security_trimming, and emits_audit_event_expectation are hard-coded false and re-verified by the validator across nine representative cases (base_user, cross_tenant, hermes_evidence, claude_review, permission_matrix, ai_decision, legal_hold, approval_route, allowed_test).

(4) Suppression: only a denylist of forbidden field NAMES is published (contract fixture_permission_matrix_reference_hidden_source_fields and catalog hidden_source_fields). No field values, internal rule predicates, ACL grant contents, or route reasons appear anywhere. Run-case results omit the denylist and assert unauthorized_count_exposed, unauthorized_object_name_exposed, and hidden_field_names_exposed are false; the validator further asserts each sensitive name (raw_document_body, secret, credential, permission_matrix_internal_rule, object_acl_internal_grant, review/approval_route_internal_reason, unauthorized_object_name, etc.) is present in the denylist.

(5) Handoff: CP00-143 terminated at RP03.P05.M06.S01 (base tenant); M06 start=2 begins at S02 with no overlap and no gap. next_pack CP00-145 and next_subphase RP03.P06.M03.S20 are bound in the pack binding, contract current_pack, and closeout handoff; M03 count=19 leaves S20 for the next pack. implements_ldip=false and splits_hrx_product=false throughout the contract, attestation, and per-result flags; HRX stays embedded and LDIP is not implemented. Contract schema bumped v0.10 to v0.11 with the validator updated in lockstep, and current_pack risk_class/unit_count/range/flags updated consistently.

Non-blocking observation: the Hermes evidence packet lists docs/closeout-packs/cp00-144/manifest.json as an evidence_ref though that file is not part of the staged diff. Both the test and the validator tolerate its absence via try/catch plus plan fallback, and the supplied validate run (current_pack CP00-144) confirms the plan already carries CP00-144, so coverage is satisfied without the manifest. No P0-P3 findings; pack and goal closeout are unblocked.
